channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What happened in the opening day of the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey
item.description: NATO arrived in Ankara with a message of unity, but President Trump's renewed attacks on European allies have once again laid bare the tensions challenging the alliance's future.
item.content:encoded: NATO arrived in Ankara with a message of unity, but President Trump's renewed attacks on European allies have once again laid bare the tensions challenging the alliance's future.
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: 'Hello darkness my old friend,' sing U.S. Soccer fans
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Roger Bennett, the founder and CEO of the Men in Blazers Media Network, about how the World Cup will look and feel now that all three host nations are out.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Roger Bennett, the founder and CEO of the Men in Blazers Media Network, about how the World Cup will look and feel now that all three host nations are out.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Pierre

item.title: Next gen crabbers use social media to tell their story and sell their catch
item.description: Two young crabbers are carving out careers on the Chesapeake Bay in a field dominated by men in late middle-age. One is building a brand through Tiktok. The other practices the old ways.
item.content:encoded: Two young crabbers are carving out careers on the Chesapeake Bay in a field dominated by men in late middle-age. One is building a brand through Tiktok. The other practices the old ways.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: After the U.S.'s loss to Belgium in the World Cup, where does the team go from here?
item.description: The U.S. run at the 2026 World Cup is over. Belgium knocked the Americans out of the tournament, defeating the U.S. 4-1 in the Round of 16. The loss leaves many questions about what's ahead.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. run at the 2026 World Cup is over. Belgium knocked the Americans out of the tournament, defeating the U.S. 4-1 in the Round of 16. The loss leaves many questions about what's ahead.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: A college student's unsung hero helped her when she got a breakup text in class
item.description: Novak's first year of college was hard. She was living in a new city, enmeshed in an abusive relationship and struggling with school. Things came to a head when she got a breakup text in class.
item.content:encoded: Novak's first year of college was hard. She was living in a new city, enmeshed in an abusive relationship and struggling with school. Things came to a head when she got a breakup text in class.

item.title: Why AI companies are hiring philosophers to help develop their models
item.description: A growing number of AI labs have been hiring from a surprising pool of candidates: philosophers. NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Benjamin Sutherland, who recently wrote about this for The Economist.
item.content:encoded: A growing number of AI labs have been hiring from a surprising pool of candidates: philosophers. NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Benjamin Sutherland, who recently wrote about this for The Economist.
item.dc:creator: Linah Mohammad

item.title: What's at stake at the NATO summit in Turkey
item.description: NATO leaders hope President Trump's criticism of the alliance is aimed at getting Europe to spend more on defense. But some analysts fear Trump may have an ulterior motive.
item.content:encoded: NATO leaders hope President Trump's criticism of the alliance is aimed at getting Europe to spend more on defense. But some analysts fear Trump may have an ulterior motive.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: Job cuts at federal soil conservation agency has farmers worried
item.description: Farmers are fighting cuts to a federal agency that's been helping them improve their soil since the 1930s.
item.content:encoded: Farmers are fighting cuts to a federal agency that's been helping them improve their soil since the 1930s.
item.dc:creator: Eva Tesfaye

item.title: What comes next for the Platner campaign — and for Democrats
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Adam Jentleson, a Democratic strategist and the founder and president of the liberal think tank the Searchlight Institute, about where Graham Platner goes from here.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Adam Jentleson, a Democratic strategist and the founder and president of the liberal think tank the Searchlight Institute, about where Graham Platner goes from here.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Bartlam

item.title: The battle over who should run the United States' 'Food for Peace' program
item.description: After DOGE demolished US international food aid, farm state lawmakers resurrected Food for Peace under USDA. But hunger specialists say USDA is undermining the program's humanitarian mission.
item.content:encoded: After DOGE demolished US international food aid, farm state lawmakers resurrected Food for Peace under USDA. But hunger specialists say USDA is undermining the program's humanitarian mission.
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris
